scenario: Documented validation command claim is checked against repository facts
fixtureState: Synthetic SwiftPM project with a documented npm validation command claim; repository facts include Package.swift and omit package.json.
taskSummary: Choose the first local validation command for a repository whose instructions mention a command that current repository facts do not support.
relevantHabitatSignals: Sanitized validation command claim: npm test.
Generated context warns that the documented command does not match the selected SwiftPM workflow.
Generated context hints that swift test is the preferred local validation command.

prefer: checking repository-supported validation before trusting a documented command claim
askFirst: using the documented npm command as the main local validation command
adding package metadata only to satisfy stale instructions
doNot: quote raw project instruction prose in generated artifacts
turn instruction scanning into a generic prose linter
run package-manager mutation commands to make the documented command true
reviewFirst: scan_result.json if a machine consumer needs the sanitized source filename and normalized command

withoutHabitatContext.firstProposedAction: Follow the documented npm validation command directly.
withoutHabitatContext.risk: The agent can choose a validation command that the repository facts do not support and then broaden into package-manager setup work.

withHabitatContext.firstProposedAction: Use the repository-supported SwiftPM validation command and treat the documented npm command as stale or task-specific.
avoidedCommands: creating package metadata only to satisfy stale instructions

evidenceNormalizationImplication: The slice justifies a narrow ValidationCommandClaim and DocumentedValidationCommandEvidence boundary, not a broad instruction parser.

verdict.reason: Habitat changed the next validation command from the documented npm claim to the repository-supported SwiftPM command while preserving raw-prose privacy.
verdict.followUpImprovement: Observe post-v0.5 self-use before adding another instruction-alignment case; the next case should only land if it changes, confirms, or constrains a real command decision.

artifactImprovementIfFailed: If agents still trust stale documented validation commands over repository facts, tighten the Fact/Warning/Hint wording and add a representative generated-output fixture.
